Hazard: Referral attached to the wrong patient
Causes: Ambiguous or partial demographics; DOB transcription error; two patients sharing name+DOB; OCR/extraction error
Clinical effect: Clinical information filed against the wrong record - wrong or delayed treatment, missed care for the intended patient
Existing controls: Identifier-first deterministic matcher; ANY match-risk reason code (DOB_MISMATCH / PARTIAL / MULTIPLE_CANDIDATES / NO_MATCH) blocks auto-create and routes to a human; AI never owns the match decision
Control evidence: services/rules-engine/src/matcher.py; services/rules-engine/src/rules.py; REF-004; REF-005; REF-006; docs/testing/phase5-acceptance.md

Causes: Same referral letter ingested twice; re-run of the bot; concurrent submission
Clinical effect: Duplicate pathway entry - double booking, wasted clinic capacity, conflicting records, patient confusion
Existing controls: Live existing-referral check in OpenMRS; DUPLICATE_REFERRAL routes to a human; write keyed idempotently on the REF-NNN source-document id; Orchestrator queue unique-reference enforcement

Causes: Urgent referral misclassified as routine; automation files it without prioritisation
Clinical effect: Delayed diagnosis or treatment of a time-critical condition - potential for serious harm
Existing controls: Urgent / suspected-cancer / red-flag detection at extraction; URGENT_RED_FLAG forces mandatory human review and is never silently auto-filed; reviewer expedites

Hazard: Record created from an AI mis-extraction
Causes: LLM hallucination or mis-read; degraded / handwritten scan; OCR error
Clinical effect: A record is created containing wrong clinical data - wrong downstream decisions
Existing controls: AI only proposes; output is JSON-schema validated; deterministic regex fallback path; LOW_EXTRACTION_CONFIDENCE routes to a human; the AI never owns the create decision

Causes: Mandatory clinical fields missing or non-substantive in the source letter
Clinical effect: An unactionable or unsafe referral enters the pathway - patient stuck, clinician cannot act
Existing controls: Completeness rules over mandatory fields; INCOMPLETE_MANDATORY_FIELDS routes to a human; reviewer may amend-and-complete or reject back to the GP

Hazard: Safeguarding / child-patient concern processed without oversight
Causes: Automation handles a paediatric or safeguarding-flagged referral end to end
Clinical effect: A safeguarding concern is not escalated to the right person - risk to a vulnerable patient
Existing controls: SAFEGUARDING and CHILD_PATIENT signals force mandatory human review; reviewer flags to the named safeguarding lead before any record is created

Causes: Corrupt file; unsupported format; read failure
Clinical effect: A referral is lost - the patient is never seen, with no record that it arrived
Existing controls: FILE_UNREADABLE raises a System Exception (not a silent skip); the queue item is marked Failed and the event is audited for follow-up; fail-safe not fail-open

Hazard: Non-referral document creates a spurious record
Causes: A letter that is not a referral is mistaken for one
Clinical effect: A meaningless or misleading record enters the pathway
Existing controls: is_referral detection; a readable non-referral raises a Business Exception (no retry, no record) and is audited

Hazard: Bot fails open and writes an unsafe record
Causes: Transient OpenMRS / service error mid-write; partial write; verification skipped
Clinical effect: A record is created but is incomplete or unverified - clinicians trust bad data
Existing controls: Every create is verified by re-reading it back from OpenMRS; a verify mismatch raises a System Exception; MaxRetryNumber and MaxConsecutiveSystemExceptions are explicit; the bot escalates rather than proceed

Hazard: A safety-relevant action cannot be audited
Causes: No trace of who/what/when - harm cannot be investigated or learned from
Clinical effect: Inability to investigate an incident, identify the cause, or demonstrate safe operation
Existing controls: Append-only audit log (data/audit/audit-log.csv) written on every system action - decision, write, route, exception; the human-review store records reviewer identity and rationale (who / what / before-after / when / why)

Hazard: Referral routed to review is never resolved
Causes: Review store grows; no clinician picks it up; a decision is lost
Clinical effect: Indefinitely delayed care for a patient whose referral is stuck in a queue
Existing controls: Persistent SQLite review store with an explicit PENDING -> DECIDED -> RESOLVED lifecycle; nothing is silently dropped; idempotent resolver; PENDING backlog is queryable and reportable

Hazard: Wrong reviewer decision applied with no accountability
Causes: A reviewer approves the wrong patient or rejects a valid referral with no record of why
Clinical effect: An unsafe outcome is actioned and cannot be traced back to a decision-maker
Existing controls: A decision requires a named reviewer and a mandatory rationale; the decision changes the outcome only through the resolver, which still verifies the OpenMRS write; the whole decision is audited
